text:
The user wants me to create a Mermaid diagram showing the design of the solar system card class. Let me first read the spec to understand the architecture, then create an .mmd file.

This is a straightforward task - create a diagram file. Let me read the spec first for reference, then write the .mmd file directly.

The user wants a Mermaid .mmd diagram illustrating the design of the solar system card. I need to create a diagram file in the canvas/ directory. Let me create a comprehensive architecture diagram showing the card class structure, dependencies, data flow, and component relationships.

I'll create `canvas/solar-system-design.mmd` with a Mermaid diagram.

input.content:
# Solar System Card — Design ## Architecture Overview ```mermaid graph TB subgraph browser["Browser — card.js (Tier 1, no sidecar)"] CARD[card.js
CARD_SHIM global] CARD -->|creates| SCENE[Three.js Scene] CARD -->|creates| RENDERER[WebGL Renderer] CARD -->|creates| CAMERA[Perspective Camera] CARD -->|controls| CTRL[OrbitControls
zoom / pan / rotate] SCENE -->|contains| SUN[Sun mesh
emissive glow] SCENE -->|contains| PLANETS[8 Planet meshes
with textures] SCENE -->|contains| RINGS[Saturn RingGeometry
ring texture] SCENE -->|contains| ORBITS[Orbital path lines
THREE.Line] SCENE -->|contains| STARS[Starfield
THREE.Points] SCENE -->|contains| LABELS[Planet name labels
HTML overlay] CARD -->|manages| ANIM[Animation loop
requestAnimationFrame] ANIM -->|updates| ORBITAL[Orbital positions
Kepler's third law] ANIM -->|updates| ROTATION[Axial rotation] CARD -->|drives| INFO[Info panel
DOM overlay] CARD -->|loads via| TEX[THREE.TextureLoader
NASA / Wikimedia URLs] TEX -->|fallback| COLOR[Procedural color sphere] CARD -->|disposes via| DESTROY|onDestroy hook end subgraph external["External resources"] CDN[Three.js 0.160.0 UMD
cdn.jsdelivr.net] CTRL_ESM[OrbitControls ESM
dynamic import] TEX_NASA[NASA / Wikimedia
public-domain textures] end CDN -->|metadata.scripts| CARD CTRL_ESM -->|dynamic import| CARD TEX_NASA -->|TextureLoader URLs| TEX classDef tier fill:#2d2d2d,stroke:#666,color:#fff; classDef external fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#444,color:#aaa; class CARD,SCENE,RENDERER,CAMERA,CTRL,SUN,PLANETS,RINGS,ORBITS,STARS,LABELS,ANIM,ORBITAL,ROTATION,INFO,TEX,COLOR,DESTROY tier; class CDN,CTRL_ESM,TEX_NASA external; ``` ## Component Breakdown ```mermaid graph LR subgraph cardClass["solar-system card class"] META[metadata.json
name: solar-system
badge: SST
handler: ~
sidecar: ~
scripts: three.min.js] HTML[card.html
fragment — empty
Three.js renders into container] CSS[card.css
overlay styles —
labels, info panel,
starfield cursor] JS[card.js
main logic —
init / animate / cleanup] end META -->|registers| CLASS HTML -->|paired with| CLASS CSS -->|paired with| CLASS JS -->|paired with| CLASS CLASS -->|instances on| CANVAS[canvas/ — .solar-system extension] class cardClass fill:#1e1e1e,stroke:#333,color:#fff; class META,HTML,CSS,JS,CANVAS fill:#252526,stroke:#444,color:#ccc; ``` ## Data Flow — Init → Animate ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant U as User participant C as card.js participant T as Three.js participant L as TextureLoader participant A as Animation loop U->>C: Card loads on canvas C->>C: Create scene, camera, renderer C->>C: Add OrbitControls C->>C: Create Sun mesh (emissive) C->>C: Create 8 planet meshes C->>C: Create Saturn ring geometry C->>C: Create orbital path lines C->>C: Create starfield points C->>C: Create HTML labels + info panel C->>L: Load textures (parallel) L-->>C: Textures loaded (or fallback) C->>T: Append scene to container C->>T: Set up resize handler loop every frame A->>C: requestAnimationFrame C->>A: Update orbital positions
Kepler's law: ω = 2π/T C->>A: Rotate planets on axis C->>T: Render scene C->>C: Update label positions end U->>C: User zooms / pans / rotates C->>C: OrbitControls handles input C->>T: Re-render with new camera ``` ## Planet Data Model ```mermaid classDiagram class PlanetConfig { +string name +float distance // semi-major axis (Earth = 10) +float period // orbital period (Earth = 1 year) +float size // sphere radius +float tilt // axial tilt in degrees +float speed // rotation speed +string textureUrl // NASA / Wikimedia URL +string color // procedural fallback } class Planet { +THREE.Mesh mesh +THREE.Group group +float phase // initial orbital offset +float angle // current orbital angle +update(time) +rotate() +dispose() } class SaturnRings { +THREE.Mesh ringMesh +RingGeometry +update() +dispose() } class Starfield { +THREE.Points points +BufferGeometry +createStars(count) +dispose() } class InfoPanel { +HTMLElement container +showPlanet(name, info) +hide() } PlanetConfig --> Planet : configures Planet : contains *-- SaturnRings PlanetConfig : 8 instances --> Planet ``` ## File Layout ```mermaid graph LR ROOT[.mica/card-classes/solar-system/] ROOT --> META[metadata.json] ROOT --> HTML[card.html] ROOT --> CSS[card.css] ROOT --> JS[card.js] CANVAS[canvas/] CANVAS --> S1[solar-system.solar-system
card instance] META -.->|extends| CLASS HTML -.->|extends| CLASS CSS -.->|extends| CLASS JS -.->|extends| CLASS class ROOT fill:#2d2d2d,stroke:#666,color:#fff; class META,HTML,CSS,JS fill:#1e1e1e,stroke:#444,color:#ccc; class CANVAS,S1 fill:#252526,stroke:#555,color:#aaa; ``` ## Key Invariants - **No sidecar** — all computation is client-side (Tier 1). No server.py / server.ts needed. - **No handler** — no LLM, no classification, no subprocess. `handler: ~` in metadata. - **Texture fallback** — if any NASA texture fails to load, a procedural colored sphere is used. No crash. - **Cleanup** — `onDestroy` disposes all Three.js geometries, materials, textures, and cancels `requestAnimationFrame`. - **No localStorage** — no persistence. Each card instance is fresh. - **No mica API calls** — the card doesn't call `mica.files`, `mica.openChannel`, or `mica.fetch`. It is purely self-contained.

text:
The user asked me to create a .mmd diagram illustrating the design of the solar system card. I've created a comprehensive Mermaid diagram file at `canvas/solar-system-design.mmd` with:

1. **Architecture Overview** - Shows the browser-side architecture with Three.js scene, camera, renderer, controls, and all visual elements
2. **Component Breakdown** - Shows the card class structure (metadata.json, card.html, card.css, card.js) and how it registers as a card class
3. **Data Flow — Init → Animate** - Sequence diagram showing the initialization and animation loop flow
4. **Planet Data Model** - Class diagram showing the data structures for planets, Saturn rings, starfield, and info panel
5. **File Layout** - Shows the directory structure of the card class and canvas instances
6. **Key Invariants** - Important constraints and design decisions

The diagram is comprehensive and covers all the key aspects of the solar system card design.
